title: pyrrocoma integrifolia
intro: Pyrrocoma integrifolia, also known as the woolly bear, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and to provide relief from pain and fever. Pyrrocoma integrifolia can be used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
